definitions.text: To make smaller; to press or squeeze together, or to make something occupy a smaller space or volume.
examples: The force required to compress a spring varies linearly with the displacement.

definitions.text: To be pressed together or folded by compression into a more economic, easier format.
examples: Our new model compresses easily, ideal for storage and travel

definitions.text: To condense into a more economic, easier format.
examples: This chart compresses the entire audit report into a few lines on a single diagram.

definitions.text: A multiply folded piece of cloth, a pouch of ice, etc., used to apply to a patient's skin, cover the dressing of wounds, and placed with the aid of a bandage to apply pressure on an injury.
examples: He held a cold compress over the sprain.
